title: grossularia alpestris var. gigantea
intro: Grossularia alpestris var. gigantea, also known as giant alpine hawthorn, is a large shrub or small tree in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include being astringent and anti-inflammatory. The health benefits of Grossularia alpestris var. gigantea include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in infusions or added to baths for its medicinal properties.
